filters.term: host=172.30.14.2 and apply_overrides=0 and levels=chmlgf and rows=1000 and min_qod=0 and sort-reverse=severity first=1

result.name: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2025) - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Aug 2025) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2025-8713: Optimizer statistics can expose sampled data within a view, partition, or child table
- CVE-2025-8714: pg_dump lets superuser of origin server execute arbitrary code in psql client
- CVE-2025-8715: pg_dump newline in object name executes arbitrary code in psql client and in restore target server|affected=PostgreSQL prior to version 13.22, 14.x prior to 14.19, 15.x prior to 15.14, 16.x prior to 16.10 and 17.x prior to 17.6.|impact=|solution=Update to version 13.22, 14.19, 15.14, 16.10, 17.6 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.4.26 Fixed version: 13.22 Installation path / port: 5432/tcp

result.name: PostgreSQL End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL End of Life (EOL) Detection - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=The PostgreSQL version on the remote host has reached the End of Life (EOL) and should not be used anymore.|insight=|affected=|impact=An EOL version of PostgreSQL is not receiving any security updates from the vendor. Unfixed security vulnerabilities might be leveraged by an attacker to compromise the security of this host.|solution=Update the PostgreSQL version on the remote host to a still supported version.|vuldetect=Checks if an EOL version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix
nvt.solution: Update the PostgreSQL version on the remote host to a still supported version.

result.description:
The "PostgreSQL" version on the remote host has reached the end of life.
CPE: cpe:/a:postgresql:postgresql:9.4.26 Installed version: 9.4.26 Location/URL: 5432/tcp EOL version: 9.4 EOL date: 2020-02-13

result.name: PostgreSQL < 10.6, 11.x < 11.1 SQL Injection Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL < 10.6, 11.x < 11.1 SQL Injection Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to an SQL injection vulnerability.|insight=A SQL Injection flaw has been discovered in PostgreSQL server in the way triggers that enable transition relations are dumped. The transition relation name is not correctly quoted and it may allow an attacker with CREATE privilege on some non-temporary schema or TRIGGER privilege on some table to create a malicious trigger that, when dumped and restored, would result in additional SQL statements being executed.|affected=PostgreSQL before versions 10.6 and 11.1.|impact=|solution=Update to version 10.6 or 11.1 respectively.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.4.26 Fixed version: 10.6 Installation path / port: 5432/tcp

result.name: PostgreSQL Default Credentials (PostgreSQL Protocol)

nvt.name: PostgreSQL Default Credentials (PostgreSQL Protocol)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:C/I:P/A:P|summary=It was possible to login into the remote PostgreSQL as user postgres using weak credentials.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=Change the password as soon as possible.|vuldetect=|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution: Change the password as soon as possible.

result.description: It was possible to login as user postgres with password "postgres".

result.name: PostgreSQL < 9.6.24, 10.x < 10.19, 11.x < 11.14, 12.x < 12.9, 13.x < 13.5, 14.x < 14.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL < 9.6.24, 10.x < 10.19, 11.x < 11.14, 12.x < 12.9, 13.x < 13.5, 14.x < 14.1 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2021-23214: Server processes unencrypted bytes from man-in-the-middle
- CVE-2021-23222: libpq processes unencrypted bytes from man-in-the-middle|affected=PostgreSQL prior to version 9.6.24, version 10.x through 10.18, 11.x through 11.13, 12.x through 12.8, 13.x through 13.4 and version 14.0.|impact=|solution=Update to version 9.6.24, 10.19, 11.14, 12.9, 13.5, 14.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.4.26 Fixed version: 9.6.24 Installation path / port: 5432/tcp

result.name: PostgreSQL SQLi Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL SQLi Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:C/I:C/A:C|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to an SQL injection (SQLi) vulnerability.|insight=Improper neutralization of quoting syntax in PostgreSQL libpq functions PQescapeLiteral(), PQescapeIdentifier(), PQescapeString(), and PQescapeStringConn() allows a database input provider to achieve SQL injection in certain usage patterns. Specifically, SQL injection requires the application to use the function result to construct input to psql, the PostgreSQL interactive terminal. Similarly, improper neutralization of quoting syntax in PostgreSQL command line utility programs allows a source of command line arguments to achieve SQL injection when client_encoding is BIG5 and server_encoding is one of EUC_TW or MULE_INTERNAL.|affected=PostgreSQL prior to version 13.19, 14.x prior to 14.16, 15.x prior to 15.11, 16.x prior to 16.7 and 17.x prior to 17.3.|impact=|solution=Update to version 13.19, 14.16, 15.11, 16.7, 17.3 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.4.26 Fixed version: 13.19 Installation path / port: 5432/tcp

result.name: PostgreSQL < 9.5.23, 9.6.x < 9.6.19, 10.x < 10.14, 11.x < 11.9, 12.x < 12.4 Search Path Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL < 9.5.23, 9.6.x < 9.6.19, 10.x < 10.14, 11.x < 11.9, 12.x < 12.4 Search Path Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to an uncontrolled search path element vulnerability in CREATE EXTENSION.|insight=|affected=PostgreSQL versions prior to 9.5.23, 9.6.x prior to 9.6.19, 10.x prior to 10.14, 11.x prior to 11.9 and 12.x prior to 12.4.|impact=When a superuser runs certain CREATE EXTENSION statements, users may be able to execute arbitrary SQL functions under the identity of that superuser. The attacker must have permission to create objects in the new extension's schema or a schema of a prerequisite extension. Not all extensions are vulnerable.|solution=Update to version 9.5.23, 9.6.19, 10.14, 11.9, 12.4 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.4.26 Fixed version: 9.5.23 Installation path / port: 5432/tcp

result.name: PostgreSQL DoS Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL DoS Vulnerability (Feb 2025) - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability due to a buffer over-read.|insight=A buffer over-read in PostgreSQL GB18030 encoding validation allows a database input provider to achieve temporary denial of service on platforms where a 1-byte over-read can elicit process termination.|affected=PostgreSQL prior to version 13.21, 14.x prior to 14.18, 15.x prior to 15.13, 16.x prior to 16.9 and 17.x prior to 17.5.|impact=|solution=Update to version 13.21, 14.18, 15.13, 16.9, 17.5 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.4.26 Fixed version: 13.21 Installation path / port: 5432/tcp

result.name: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Nov 2025) - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL Multiple Vulnerabilities (Nov 2025) - Linux
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:C|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.|insight=The following flaws exist:
- CVE-2025-12817: CREATE STATISTICS does not check for schema CREATE privilege
- CVE-2025-12818: libpq undersizes allocations, via integer wraparound|affected=PostgreSQL prior to version 13.23, 14.x prior to 14.20, 15.x prior to 15.15, 16.x prior to 16.11, 17.x prior to 17.7 and 18.x prior to 18.1.|impact=|solution=Update to version 13.23, 14.20, 15.15, 16.11, 17.7, 18.1 or later.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.4.26 Fixed version: 13.23 Installation path / port: 5432/tcp

result.name: PostgreSQL < 9.5.25, 9.6.x < 9.6.21, 10.x < 10.16, 11.x < 11.11, 12.x < 12.6, 13.x < 13.2 Information Disclosure Vulnerability - Linux

nvt.name: PostgreSQL < 9.5.25, 9.6.x < 9.6.21, 10.x < 10.16, 11.x < 11.11, 12.x < 12.6, 13.x < 13.2 Information Disclosure Vulnerability - Linux
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:L/I:N/A:N|summary=PostgreSQL is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability.|insight=A user with SELECT privileges on a single column can craft a specific query to read all information from all columns of the table.|affected=PostgreSQL through version 9.5.24 and versions 9.6.0 through 9.6.20, 10.0 through 10.15, 11.0 through 11.10, 12.0 through 12.6 and 13.0 through 13.1.|impact=Successful exploitation would allow an attacker to read sensitive information.|solution=Update to version 9.5.25, 9.6.21, 10.16, 11.11, 12.6 or 13.2 respectively.|vuldetect=Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.|solution_type=VendorFix

result.description: Installed version: 9.4.26 Fixed version: 9.5.25 Installation path / port: 5432/tcp

result.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure

nvt.name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:H/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.|insight=The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as defined by RFC1323/RFC7323.|affected=TCP implementations that implement RFC1323/RFC7323.|impact=A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.|solution=To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.|vuldetect=Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.
The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.

result.description:
It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 2792712973 Packet 2: 2792714081

result.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure

nvt.name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:L/AC:L/Au:N/C:P/I:N/A:N|summary=The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.|insight=The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.|affected=|impact=This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.|solution=Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)|vuldetect=Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.|solution_type=Mitigation
nvt.solution:
Various mitigations are possible:
- Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely
- Protect the remote host by a firewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the firewall in either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)

result.description: The following response / ICMP packet has been received: - ICMP Type: 14 - ICMP Code: 0

result.name: Hostname Determination Reporting

nvt.name: Hostname Determination Reporting
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=The script reports information on how the hostname of the target was determined.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Hostname determination for IP 172.30.14.2:
Hostname|Source postgres-old.trabalho_vulnnet|Reverse-DNS

result.name: PostgreSQL Detection (TCP)

nvt.name: PostgreSQL Detection (TCP)
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=TCP based detection of PostgreSQL.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=The script sends a connection request to the server (user:postgres, DB:postgres) and attempts to extract the version number from the reply.|solution_type=

result.description: A PostgreSQL service has been identified on this port.

result.name: PostgreSQL Detection Consolidation

nvt.name: PostgreSQL Detection Consolidation
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Consolidation of PostgreSQL detections.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Detected PostgreSQL
Version: 9.4.26 Location: 5432/tcp CPE: cpe:/a:postgresql:postgresql:9.4.26
Concluded from version/product identification result: select version(); query result: T versionDPostgreSQL 9.4.26 on x86_64-pc-linux-gnu (Debian 9.4.26-1.pgdg90+1), compiled by gcc (Debian 6.3.0-18+deb9u1) 6.3.0 20170516, 64-bitCSELECT 1ZI

result.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting

nvt.name: OS Detection Consolidation and Reporting
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This script consolidates the OS information detected by several VTs and tries to find the best matching OS.
Furthermore it reports all previously collected information leading to this best matching OS. It also reports possible additional information which might help to improve the OS detection.
If any of this information is wrong or could be improved please consider to report these to the referenced community forum.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description:
Best matching OS:
OS: Linux Kernel CPE: cpe:/o:linux:kernel Found by VT: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.102002 (Operating System (OS) Detection (ICMP)) Concluded from ICMP based OS fingerprint Setting key "Host/runs_unixoide" based on this information

result.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check

nvt.name: IP Forwarding Enabled - Active Check
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:A/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Checks if the remote host has IP forwarding enabled.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=Sends a crafted Local Link Layer (LLL) frame and checks the response.|solution_type=

result.description: It was possible to route a TCP packet through the target host and received an answer which means IP forwarding is enabled.

result.name: Traceroute

nvt.name: Traceroute
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.|insight=For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.|solution_type=

result.description:
Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.30.14.2):
172.30.0.2 172.30.14.2
Network distance between scanner and target: 2

result.name: CPE Inventory

nvt.name: CPE Inventory
nvt.tags:
cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This routine uses information collected by other routines about CPE identities of operating systems, services and applications detected during the scan.
Note: Some CPEs for specific products might show up twice or more in the output. Background:
After a product got renamed or a specific vendor was acquired by another one it might happen that a product gets a new CPE within the NVD CPE Dictionary but older entries are kept with the older CPE.|insight=|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: 172.30.14.2|cpe:/a:postgresql:postgresql:9.4.26 172.30.14.2|cpe:/o:linux:kernel

result.name: Services

nvt.name: Services
nvt.tags: cvss_base_vector=AV:N/AC:L/Au:N/C:N/I:N/A:N|summary=This plugin performs service detection.|insight=This plugin attempts to guess which service is running on the remote port(s). For instance, it searches for a web server which could listen on another port than 80 or 443 and makes this information available for other check routines.|affected=|impact=|solution=|vuldetect=|solution_type=

result.description: An unknown service is running on this port. It is usually reserved for Postgres

nvt.name: Generic HTTP Directory Traversal / File Inclusion (Web Root) - Active Check
